LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. APPEARANCE FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY & FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY. NO ARGUMENT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ALLEGES A DISMISSAL WAS FILED AS TO MOVING PARTY. THERE BEING NO DISMISSAL RECEIVED BY MOVING PARTY-DEFENDANT, NOR LODGED WITH DEPT. 302, THE COURT CONDITIONALLY ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND GRANTS MOTION. IF PLAINTIFF CONFIRMS THAT DEFENDANT METALCLAD WAS DISMISSED, THEN THE TENTATIVE RULING IS VACATED AND THE MOTION IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. ORDER SIGNED, BUT NOT FILED. HON. RONALD E. QUIDACHAY. REPORTER: IRENE BURNS, CSR #1815

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Maria A. Yuen, Esq., Colombatto, Klimenko & Rosse, 415-391-6182, for defendant/moving party Metalclad.
Stephen M. Fishback, Esq., Paul, Hanley & Harley, 50-559-9980, for plaintiff/opposing party Harry Fred Wartnick.
*Appearances. No argument. Plaintiff's counsel alleges a dismissal was filed as to moving party. There being no dismissal received by moving party-defendant, nor lodged with Dept. 302, the Court conditionally adopts its tentative ruling and grants DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. If plaintiff confirms that defendant Metalclad was dismissed, then the tentative ruling is vacated and the motion is ordered off calendar. Order signed, but not filed. CSR: IRENE BURNS, #1815 (REQ/302/JU)
